favorite_teams.description: ESPN team abbreviations, used as the fallback game (scheduled/recent) when nothing is live, and as the rotation filter if show_favorite_teams_only is true

game_rotation_seconds.description: How many seconds to show each game in rotation before switching to the next one -- applies to live, past, and upcoming games alike, not just live ones. NOT the same as display_duration below (that's how long this whole plugin shows before the broader LEDMatrix system switches to a different plugin entirely).

display_duration.description: Seconds to show this ENTIRE plugin before the LEDMatrix system's own rotation switches to a different plugin. Distinct from game_rotation_seconds above, which cycles between games WITHIN this plugin while it's showing.

update_interval_seconds.description: How often to poll ESPN when nothing is live

live_update_interval_seconds.description: How often to poll ESPN while games are live

show_favorite_teams_only.description: STRICT mode: never show any other team's games at all (live, past, or upcoming) -- always favorites only. When off (default), favorites still take priority whenever they have a live game, but other teams' live games and past/upcoming games can fill the rotation when no favorite is currently playing.

live_game_exclusive.description: When a favorite team is live, show ONLY that live game -- no past/upcoming games at all, not even that same team's own. Off by default: normally, a live favorite's own past/upcoming games still show alongside it (but a DIFFERENT favorite's past/upcoming stays excluded while any favorite is live, regardless of this setting).

past_upcoming_all_teams.description: Only relevant when NOTHING is currently live anywhere (not just when no favorite is live) -- as soon as any game goes live, favorite or not, past/upcoming games are always restricted to favorites only regardless of this setting. When nothing is live: controls whether past/upcoming games expand to include all teams (true) or stay scoped to favorite teams only (false, default). Ignored (always favorites-only) when show_favorite_teams_only is on.

live_priority.description: Read directly by the LEDMatrix framework's own BasePlugin.has_live_priority() -- when true, and a favorite team is currently live (has_live_content()), the display controller stays on THIS plugin instead of rotating to a different plugin, exactly like the football-scoreboard plugin's score-celebration takeover. Distinct from everything else in this file: those settings control what shows WITHIN this plugin; this one controls whether the broader system rotates AWAY from this plugin at all. Off by default since it changes cross-plugin behavior -- opt in deliberately.

show_past_games.description: Include recently completed favorite-team games in the rotation, showing the final score with the winning team highlighted in yellow and "FINAL" on the black half.

max_past_games.description: Maximum number of past (final) favorite-team games to include in rotation

past_games_lookback_days.description: How many days back to explicitly check for a favorite team's completed games. ESPN's scoreboard endpoint only returns TODAY's games by default, so this queries additional past days individually (one extra request per day) to actually find a team's most recent finished game if it wasn't today.

past_games_refresh_seconds.description: How often to re-run the past-days lookback (separate from the main scoreboard poll interval, since completed games don't change).

show_upcoming_games.description: Include upcoming scheduled favorite-team games in the rotation (in start-time order), showing "UPCOMING" plus the date/time on the black half. No score is shown since the game hasn't started.

max_upcoming_games.description: Maximum number of upcoming favorite-team games to include in rotation

upcoming_games_lookahead_days.description: How many days ahead to explicitly check for upcoming favorite-team games. ESPN's scoreboard endpoint only returns TODAY's games by default, so this queries additional future days individually (one extra request per day) to actually find a team's next scheduled game.

upcoming_games_refresh_seconds.description: How often to re-run the future-days lookahead (separate from the main scoreboard poll interval, since schedules barely change within a day -- no need to re-check every 15 seconds during a live game).

show_logos.description: Show each team's logo at the top of its column

logo_dir.description: Path (relative to the LEDMatrix install root) to bundled team logo PNGs, named e.g. PHI.png. Falls back to downloading from ESPN if a team's file isn't found here.

use_team_colors.description: Pull each team's real primary color from ESPN instead of using the away_color/home_color fallbacks below. When this is on (the default), away_color/home_color are ONLY used if ESPN's color data is unavailable for some reason -- they won't override real team colors just because you've set them.

away_color.description: RGB fallback color for the away team column -- only actually used when use_team_colors is off, or when ESPN doesn't provide a color for that team.

home_color.description: RGB fallback color for the home team column -- only actually used when use_team_colors is off, or when ESPN doesn't provide a color for that team.

base_fill_color.description: RGB color for an occupied base on the diamond indicator

base_empty_color.description: RGB color for an empty base outline on the diamond indicator

out_fill_color.description: RGB color for a recorded out square

out_empty_color.description: RGB color for a not-yet-recorded out square outline

show_batter_name.description: Show the current batter (first initial + last name) at the bottom-right of the black half, next to the ball-strike count. Depends on ESPN actually providing batter data in the scoreboard response -- silently omitted if not available.

show_pitcher_name.description: Show the current pitcher's name at the top of the black half. Independent of show_pitch_count below -- can show one without the other.

show_pitch_count.description: Show the current pitcher's pitch count ("P:47"). Independent of show_pitcher_name above -- if both are on, shows "P:47 T.Skubal" combined; if only this is on, shows just "P:47".

show_delayed_overlay.description: Replace the black half with "DELAYED" in red when a live game is in a rain delay or other stoppage, instead of showing the (stale/frozen) normal live info.

show_last_play.description: Temporarily replace the black half with the last play description when something significant happens (hits, walks, strikeouts, outs, runs). Skips routine ball/strike/foul updates.

last_play_display_seconds.description: How many seconds the last-play overlay stays up before reverting to the normal layout

last_play_filter.description: significant = only flash for hits/walks/strikeouts/outs/runs (skips routine ball/strike/foul updates). all = flash for every single play update including pitch-by-pitch.

last_play_favorites_only.description: Restrict the last-play flash/interrupt system to favorite-team games only. Different from show_favorite_teams_only above: that controls which games appear in rotation at all; this controls which games are allowed to INTERRUPT that rotation with a flash. Useful when show_favorite_teams_only is off (all live games rotate normally) but you only want YOUR team's big moments to interrupt that rotation, not every other simultaneous game's.

show_home_run_animation.description: Play the full 3-phase home run animation (ball arc, strobe, fireworks+scrolling text) instead of the plain last-play text overlay when a home run is detected. When off, home runs just use the regular last-play flash like any other significant play, and use last_play_display_seconds instead of home_run_display_seconds for their duration.

home_run_display_seconds.description: How long the home run animation plays, independent of last_play_display_seconds -- the 3-phase animation (ball arc, strobe, fireworks+scrolling text) needs more time than a plain text flash to play out fully.

test_mode.description: Render a fake in-progress game instead of calling ESPN (for layout testing)
